parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-769 (descriptor-only Migration Platform UI fixture foundation pack; RP25.P04.M06.S03–RP25.P05.M02.S14; 150 units; risk C). VERIFIED via Read/Grep/Glob: (1) All 6 required CP769 exports exist and are wired — MIGRATION_PLATFORM_CP769_PACK_BINDING/_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js), createMigrationPlatformCp769UiFixtureFoundationCaseSet/Descriptor (service.js:991/995), validateMigrationPlatformCp769UiFixtureFoundationCoverage/Descriptor (validators.js:1174/1202), re-exported via index.js. (2) CP769 descriptor/case-set/validators follow the established CP756–768 pattern with comprehensive no-leak guards (descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, no raw/source/credential/real-client payloads, no source-system runtime opening, no cross-tenant, no permission-decision/audit-body exposure, no runtime-render claim, unauthorized_records_omitted, no unverified knowledge creation). (3) Authority boundary embedded correctly in createDescriptor: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-770 — Claude remains read-only non-final reviewer. (4) MIGRATION_PLATFORM_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION applied universally; manifest runtime_ready=false; Hermes packets descriptor-only (emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape=descriptor_only_migration_no_write) with no product-runtime embedding. (5) contracts/migration-platform-contract.json carries CP769 as latest_pack/latest_projection, preserves all historical projections (cp756–cp768), top-level validation.valid=true, and contains zero valid:false. (6) packages/migration/test/model.test.js has exactly 43 test() blocks (matches reported node --test 43/43) including CP769 binding/coverage, descriptor runtime-closed, and contract-projection-latest tests; no TODO/FIXME/real-data/placeholder markers in src. (7) Deliverable distribution {ui:43,claude_review:13,security_audit:14,implementation:41,fixture:25,test:8,hermes_evidence:6}=150 and micro-title distribution match the baseline plan and registry requirements exactly. (8) next-pack-queue.json/closeout-pack-plan.json consistent: live_latest=CP00-769, next CP00-770 / RP25.P05.M02.S15. (9) CP178+ hardening posture correct — manifest/construction-inspection/adjudication/command-evidence/claude-review-result all consistently show production_ready=false pending exactly one valid hardened read-only review; the prior 0-byte placeholder result is correctly NOT counted (review_receipt_ref=null, invalid_review_attempts=[]); readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON. PROVENANCE: command-evidence exit codes (all 0) and test counts (model 43/43, full suite 2991/2991) are agent-reported and could not be re-executed under the read-only/no-Bash constraint, but are corroborated by static source/contract/test inspection. CONCLUSION: the pack-level production_ready claim (scoped to migration_platform_ui_fixture_foundation_descriptor_verified) is supported by implementation, tests, local/Hermes validation evidence, inspection, adjudication, and no-write/no-real-data boundaries. No P0–P2 findings; closeout-eligible. Enterprise-trust/runtime authority remains with the human final approver, which is the expected boundary outside this receipt (pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust).
